content: Diagnosis requires excisional lymph node biopsy (preferred over core needle/FNA to preserve architecture). Pathology: immunohistochemistry determines lineage and subtype (B-cell: CD20, CD79a; T-cell: CD3, CD5; HL: CD30, CD15, PAX5); FISH for translocations (MYC, BCL2, BCL6 for 'double-hit' lymphoma); gene expression profiling for some subtypes. Staging: Ann Arbor system with Cotswolds modifications - Stage I (single node region), II (≥2 regions same side of diaphragm), III (both sides of diaphragm), IV (extranodal involvement); A/B symptoms (fever, night sweats, weight loss >10%). PET-CT standard for staging and response assessment (Deauville score 1-5); bone marrow biopsy often unnecessary if PET negative for HL and DLBCL. Prognostic scores: IPI (International Prognostic Index) for DLBCL (age, stage, LDH, performance status, extranodal sites); FLIPI for follicular lymphoma; IPS for advanced HL. LDH elevation indicates high tumor burden.
